dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6793 IH: Public Shipyard Workforce Protection Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Goodlander (for herself, Mrs. Kiggans of Virginia, Mr. Pappas, Ms. Tokuda, and Mr. Scott of Virginia) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To prohibit the use of funds authorized to be appropriated for the Department of Defense to carry out a hiring freeze, reduction in force, or hiring delay without cause at a public shipyard.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Public Shipyard Workforce Protection Act of 2025.

section.header: Prohibition on the use of funds from carrying out a hiring freeze, reduction in force, or hiring delay without cause at a public shipyard
section.text: None of the funds authorized to be appropriated or otherwise made available for fiscal year 2026 for the Department of Defense may be used to—

paragraph.text: carry out a hiring freeze at a public shipyard;
carry out a reduction in force at a public shipyard; or
delay without cause the filling of a vacant Federal civilian employee position at a public shipyard.
